examLabel: TGAT1 91 การสื่อสารภาษาอังกฤษ (English Communication)

speakingQR.prompt: A: "Thank you so much for helping me move today."
options: No problem at all. I was happy to help.
Yes, I am moving next week.
speakingQR.explanation_th: เมื่อมีคนขอบคุณ ควรตอบรับอย่างสุภาพ เช่น No problem / You're welcome

speakingQR.prompt: A: "Would you mind if I opened the window?"
options: No, you can never open it.
speakingQR.explanation_th: Would you mind if...? ตอบรับว่า 'ไม่ขัดข้อง' ใช้ Not at all / Of course not

speakingQR.prompt: A: "I'm afraid I can't come to your party on Saturday."
options: Oh, that's a shame. Maybe next time.
Great, see you there on Saturday!
You can't be afraid of a party.
speakingQR.explanation_th: เมื่ออีกฝ่ายปฏิเสธคำเชิญ ควรแสดงความเสียดายอย่างสุภาพ

speakingQR.prompt: A: "How was your trip to Chiang Mai?"
options: It was wonderful, thanks. We had a great time.
Chiang Mai is in the north.
speakingQR.explanation_th: ถามถึงทริปที่ผ่านมา (was) ต้องตอบด้วยอดีตและบอกความรู้สึก

speakingQR.prompt: A: "Excuse me, could you tell me how to get to the station?"
options: Sure. Go straight and turn left at the corner.
No, I can't tell you anything.
speakingQR.explanation_th: ขอเส้นทาง ควรให้คำแนะนำเส้นทางจริง ไม่ใช่ตอบ yes/no ลอย ๆ

speakingQR.prompt: A: "I've got a terrible headache."
options: Oh no. Why don't you take a rest?
speakingQR.explanation_th: เมื่ออีกฝ่ายไม่สบาย ควรแสดงความห่วงใย/แนะนำ ไม่ใช่ยินดี

speakingQR.prompt: A: "Do you fancy going to the cinema tonight?"
options: Sure, that sounds great. What's on?
No, I don't have a fancy.
speakingQR.explanation_th: fancy doing = อยากทำไหม ตอบรับด้วยความสนใจและถามต่อได้

speakingQR.prompt: A: "I'm really sorry, but I broke your mug."
options: Don't worry about it. It was old anyway.
Thank you for the new mug.
Yes, you broke it on purpose.
I'm sorry for your mug too.
speakingQR.explanation_th: เมื่อมีคนขอโทษเรื่องเล็กน้อย ควรให้อภัยอย่างใจดี

speakingQR.prompt: A: "Congratulations on passing your driving test!"
options: Thanks a lot! I was so nervous about it.
speakingQR.explanation_th: เมื่อได้รับคำแสดงยินดี ควรขอบคุณ

speakingQR.prompt: A: "Shall I carry that bag for you? It looks heavy."
options: Oh, thank you. That's very kind of you.
No, the bag is as light as air.
Yes, you shall not carry it.
speakingQR.explanation_th: Shall I...? เป็นการเสนอช่วยเหลือ ตอบรับด้วยการขอบคุณอย่างสุภาพ

speakingQR.prompt: A: "I love your new haircut! It looks great."
options: Thank you! That's so kind of you to say.
You should cut your hair too.
speakingQR.explanation_th: ตอบรับคำชมด้วยการขอบคุณ

speakingQR.prompt: A: "The meeting is at half past ten in room 304."
options: Sorry, could you repeat the room number?
Half past ten is a time.
speakingQR.explanation_th: ขอให้พูดซ้ำเมื่อฟังไม่ชัด

speakingQR.prompt: A: "Would you like some more coffee?"
speakingQR.explanation_th: ปฏิเสธการเสนอเครื่องดื่มอย่างสุภาพ

speakingQR.prompt: A: "I'm so bored this afternoon."
options: Why don't we go for a walk?
speakingQR.explanation_th: เสนอกิจกรรมเมื่ออีกฝ่ายเบื่อ

speakingQR.prompt: A: "Let's order pizza for dinner tonight."
speakingQR.explanation_th: ตอบรับข้อเสนออย่างกระตือรือร้น

speakingQR.prompt: A: "Hello, could I speak to Mr. Brown, please?"
options: Just a moment, I'll put you through.
speakingQR.explanation_th: สำนวนรับโทรศัพท์: โอนสายให้

speakingQR.prompt: A: "You're late again! The meeting started ten minutes ago."
options: I'm so sorry. The traffic was terrible.
speakingQR.explanation_th: ขอโทษเมื่อมาสาย พร้อมเหตุผล

speakingQR.prompt: A: "Is it OK if I leave a bit early today?"
speakingQR.explanation_th: อนุญาตเมื่อถูกขอ

speakingQR.prompt: A: "Hi, I'm Nina. Nice to meet you."
options: Nice to meet you too. I'm Sam.
speakingQR.explanation_th: แนะนำตัวตอบกลับเมื่อพบกันครั้งแรก

speakingQR.prompt: A: "How far is the post office from here?"
options: Far is the opposite of near.
speakingQR.explanation_th: บอกระยะทาง/เวลาเดิน

speakingQR.prompt: A: "Are you ready to order, or do you need a few more minutes?"
options: Could we have a few more minutes, please?
speakingQR.explanation_th: ขอเวลาดูเมนูเพิ่ม

speakingQR.prompt: A: "I failed my driving test again."
options: Oh, I'm sorry to hear that. Better luck next time.
speakingQR.explanation_th: แสดงความเห็นใจเมื่อได้ข่าวร้าย

speakingQR.prompt: A: "Would you rather have tea or coffee?"
options: Coffee and tea are both drinks.
speakingQR.explanation_th: เลือกสิ่งที่ชอบเมื่อถูกถาม

speakingQR.prompt: A: "How's your sister doing these days?"
options: She's doing really well, thanks for asking.
speakingQR.explanation_th: ตอบถามสารทุกข์คนในครอบครัว

speakingQR.prompt: A: "Is everything all right with your room, sir?"
options: Actually, the air conditioner isn't working.
Yes, the room is a room.
No, I don't have a room.
speakingQR.explanation_th: ร้องเรียนปัญหาอย่างสุภาพ

speakingQR.prompt: A: "You look lost. Can I help you find something?"
options: Yes, please. I'm looking for the train station.
No, I am not a thing.
speakingQR.explanation_th: ตอบรับความช่วยเหลือ

speakingQR.prompt: A: "What time works best for you to meet tomorrow?"
options: How about two o'clock in the afternoon?
speakingQR.explanation_th: เสนอเวลานัด

speakingQR.prompt: A: "Guess what? I got the job!"
options: I'm so sorry to hear that.
A job is a kind of work.
speakingQR.explanation_th: ยินดีกับข่าวดี

speakingQR.prompt: A: "I'd better get going. It was lovely to see you."
options: You too! Take care and see you soon.
speakingQR.explanation_th: กล่าวลาตอบกลับ

speakingQR.prompt: A: "Shall we split the bill?"
options: No, bills are made of paper.
speakingQR.explanation_th: ตอบรับการหารค่าอาหาร

speakingQR.prompt: A: "What do you think of the new café on Main Street?"
options: I really like it. The coffee is excellent.
speakingQR.explanation_th: แสดงความคิดเห็น

speakingQR.prompt: A: "I'm really nervous about my interview tomorrow."
options: Don't worry, you'll do great. Just be yourself.
speakingQR.explanation_th: ปลอบใจ/ให้กำลังใจ

speakingQR.prompt: A: "We're having a barbecue on Sunday. Would you like to join us?"
options: I'd love to! Thanks for inviting me.
speakingQR.explanation_th: ตอบรับคำเชิญ

speakingQR.prompt: A: "Could you possibly water my plants while I'm away?"
options: Of course, no problem at all.
speakingQR.explanation_th: ตอบรับเมื่อถูกขอความช่วยเหลือ

speakingQR.prompt: A: "So, we'll meet at the entrance at noon, right?"
options: Yes, that's right. See you there.
speakingQR.explanation_th: ยืนยันนัดหมาย

speakingQR.prompt: A: "There you go — I've fixed your computer."
options: Thank you so much! You're a lifesaver.
speakingQR.explanation_th: ขอบคุณเมื่อได้รับความช่วยเหลือ

speakingQR.prompt: A: "Would you like to come to the concert with us?"
options: I'd love to, but I'm afraid I'm busy that night.
speakingQR.explanation_th: ปฏิเสธคำเชิญอย่างสุภาพ พร้อมเหตุผล

speakingQR.prompt: A: "Excuse me, where's the nearest ATM?"
options: There's one just around the corner, next to the bank.
No, I will not go there.
speakingQR.explanation_th: บอกตำแหน่งสถานที่

speakingQR.prompt: A: "Thanks for the ride home!"
options: Home is a kind of place.
speakingQR.explanation_th: ตอบรับคำขอบคุณแบบเป็นกันเอง

speakingQR.prompt: A: "Lovely weather today, isn't it?"
options: Yes, it's beautiful. Perfect for a walk.
speakingQR.explanation_th: คุยเล่นเรื่องอากาศ (small talk)

shortConversations.title: At a café
shortConversations.text:
Waiter:  Good afternoon. Are you ready to order?
Customer: __(1)__
Waiter:  Sure. Would you like anything to drink with that?
Customer: __(2)__
Waiter:  Of course. __(3)__
Customer: Thank you.

options: Yes, I'll have the chicken sandwich, please.
No, I am the waiter here.
questions.explanation_th: ลูกค้าพร้อมสั่ง ควรบอกเมนูที่ต้องการ

options: Just a glass of water, thanks.
Yes, I am thirsty for nothing.
No drinks for me ever again.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบคำถามเรื่องเครื่องดื่มด้วยสิ่งที่ต้องการ

options: You are not allowed to have water.
I do not actually work here.
questions.explanation_th: พนักงานรับคำสั่งแล้ว ควรบอกว่าจะนำมาให้

shortConversations.title: Making weekend plans
shortConversations.text:
Ann: Hi Mali, __(1)__
Mali: I'm good, thanks. Hey, are you free this weekend?
Ann: __(2)__ Why?
Mali: There's a new art exhibition downtown. __(3)__
Ann: That sounds great! __(4)__
Mali: Let's meet at ten on Saturday.

questions.explanation_th: ทักทายเพื่อน ควรถามสารทุกข์
ตอบว่าว่างหรือไม่

options: You are not allowed to come.
questions.explanation_th: ชวนเพื่อนไปด้วย

options: I really don't want to meet.
questions.explanation_th: ตกลงไปแล้ว ควรถามเวลานัด

shortConversations.title: At the library desk
shortConversations.text:
Student:   Excuse me, I'm looking for books on Thai history. __(1)__
Librarian: Certainly. They're on the second floor, in section H.
Student:   __(2)__
Librarian: Yes, you can borrow up to five at a time.
Student:   __(3)__
Librarian: You're welcome. Enjoy your reading.

options: Could you help me find them?
I really don't need any help.
History is a very old subject.
questions.explanation_th: ขอความช่วยเหลือหาหนังสือ

options: No, I will not borrow them.
The floor is the second one.
questions.explanation_th: ถามเรื่องการยืม

options: No thanks for nothing at all.
I will not read any of them.
You are welcome too, I guess.
questions.explanation_th: จบบทสนทนาด้วยการขอบคุณ

shortConversations.title: At a pharmacy
shortConversations.text:
Pharmacist: Hello, how can I help you today?
Customer:   __(1)__
Pharmacist: I see. How long have you had these symptoms?
Customer:   __(2)__
Pharmacist: I'd recommend this syrup. Take it twice a day.
Customer:   __(3)__
Pharmacist: That'll be 120 baht.

options: I have a sore throat and a cough.
questions.explanation_th: บอกอาการเมื่อเภสัชกรถาม

options: No, I won't tell you that.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบว่ามีอาการมานานเท่าไร (How long)

options: Thank you. How much is it?
questions.explanation_th: ขอบคุณแล้วถามราคา

shortConversations.title: At the lost and found
shortConversations.text:
Visitor: Excuse me, I think I left my umbrella here yesterday.
Staff:   __(1)__
Visitor: __(2)__
Staff:   Let me check. ... Yes, is this the one?
Visitor: __(3)__

options: Can you describe it for me?
questions.explanation_th: เจ้าหน้าที่ขอให้อธิบายลักษณะของหาย

options: It's a large black one with a wooden handle.
questions.explanation_th: อธิบายลักษณะของที่หาย

options: Yes, that's it! Thank you so much.
questions.explanation_th: ยืนยันว่าใช่ของตน แล้วขอบคุณ

shortConversations.title: Hotel check-in
shortConversations.text:
Receptionist: Good evening. Do you have a reservation?
Guest:        __(1)__
Receptionist: Let me see... a double room for two nights. Is that correct?
Guest:        __(2)__
Receptionist: Could I see your passport, please?
Guest:        __(3)__
Receptionist: Thank you. Here's your key. Breakfast is from seven to ten.
Guest:        __(4)__

questions.explanation_th: ยืนยันการจองพร้อมบอกชื่อ

options: Two nights is a long time.
questions.explanation_th: ยืนยันรายละเอียดห้องพัก

options: I don't have a face today.
questions.explanation_th: ยื่นพาสปอร์ตเมื่อถูกขอ

options: Great. What floor is the room on?
Keys are used to open doors.
questions.explanation_th: ถามต่อเรื่องชั้นของห้อง

shortConversations.title: Making an appointment by phone
shortConversations.text:
Receptionist: Good morning, Dr. Lee's clinic. How can I help?
Caller:       __(1)__
Receptionist: Of course. Would Thursday at 3 p.m. suit you?
Caller:       __(2)__
Receptionist: Yes, five o'clock is fine. May I have your name?
Caller:       __(3)__

options: I'd like to make an appointment, please.
questions.explanation_th: บอกจุดประสงค์: นัดหมาย

options: Could we make it a little later, around five?
Yes, three is an odd number.
questions.explanation_th: ขอเลื่อนเวลานัดอย่างสุภาพ
บอกชื่อเมื่อถูกถาม

shortConversations.title: At a clothing shop
shortConversations.text:
Assistant: Hi! Are you looking for anything in particular?
Customer:  __(1)__
Assistant: Sure. What size are you?
Customer:  __(2)__
Assistant: How about this blue one? Would you like to try it on?
Customer:  __(3)__
Assistant: They're just over there, on the right.
Customer:  __(4)__

options: Yes, I'm looking for a jacket.
No, I am a shop assistant.
questions.explanation_th: บอกสิ่งที่กำลังมองหา

options: Yes, I am a size indeed.
questions.explanation_th: บอกไซซ์เมื่อถูกถาม

options: Yes, where are the fitting rooms?
questions.explanation_th: ตอบรับแล้วถามที่ลองเสื้อ

options: Thanks. Do you have it in black as well?
No, the right is a direction.
questions.explanation_th: ถามต่อเรื่องสีอื่น

shortConversations.title: Asking a classmate for notes
shortConversations.text:
Mali: Hi, I was absent yesterday. __(1)__
Ben:  Yes, we have to read chapter four and answer the questions.
Mali: __(2)__
Ben:  Sure, I'll send you a photo of them tonight.
Mali: __(3)__

questions.explanation_th: ถามเรื่องการบ้านที่พลาดไป

options: Could I borrow your notes from the lesson?
questions.explanation_th: ขอยืมโน้ตเพื่อน

options: That would be great. Thanks a lot!
questions.explanation_th: ขอบคุณเมื่อเพื่อนช่วย

shortConversations.title: At the train ticket counter
shortConversations.text:
Clerk:     Next, please. Where would you like to go?
Passenger: __(1)__
Clerk:     Single or return?
Passenger: __(2)__
Clerk:     When would you like to travel back?
Passenger: __(3)__
Clerk:     That'll be 850 baht in total.
Passenger: __(4)__

options: I'd like a ticket to Chiang Mai, please.
No, I am the ticket clerk.
questions.explanation_th: บอกจุดหมายปลายทาง

options: No, tickets are made of paper.
questions.explanation_th: เลือกประเภทตั๋ว
บอกวันเดินทางกลับ

options: Here you are. Which platform is it?
questions.explanation_th: จ่ายเงินแล้วถามชานชาลา

shortConversations.title: Inviting a friend over
shortConversations.text:
Ann: Hey, are you doing anything on Friday evening?
Tom: __(1)__
Ann: I'm having a few friends over for dinner. Would you like to come?
Tom: __(2)__
Ann: Just yourself! But a dessert would be nice if you like.
Tom: __(3)__

options: Not that I know of. Why?
questions.explanation_th: ตอบว่าว่าง แล้วถามเหตุผล

options: I'd love to! What can I bring?
questions.explanation_th: ตอบรับคำเชิญ แล้วถามว่าเอาอะไรไป

options: Perfect. I'll make a cake then.
questions.explanation_th: ตกลงจะทำของหวานไป

shortConversations.title: A problem at a restaurant
shortConversations.text:
Waiter:   Is everything OK with your meal?
Customer: __(1)__
Waiter:   Oh, I'm so sorry. I'll heat it up for you right away.
Customer: __(2)__
Waiter:   Of course. I'll bring that as well.
Customer: __(3)__

options: Actually, my soup is a bit cold.
questions.explanation_th: แจ้งปัญหาอาหารอย่างสุภาพ

options: Thank you. Could I also have some bread?
I won't eat any of it.
questions.explanation_th: ขอบคุณแล้วขอเพิ่มอีกอย่าง
ขอบคุณเมื่อพนักงานช่วยเหลือ

longConversations.title: At a travel agency
longConversations.text:
Agent:    Good morning. How can I help you today?
Customer: __(1)__
Agent:    Certainly. Where would you like to go?
Customer: __(2)__
Agent:    A beach holiday sounds lovely. How long are you planning to stay?
Customer: __(3)__
Agent:    For a week, I'd recommend Krabi. It has beautiful beaches and good hotels.
Customer: __(4)__
Agent:    The package starts at 8,000 baht per person, including flights and hotel.
Customer: __(5)__
Agent:    Wonderful. I'll prepare the booking for you.

options: I'd like to book a holiday, please.
No, I am just the agent.
questions.explanation_th: บอกจุดประสงค์ที่มา

options: Somewhere warm with a nice beach.
I don't want to go anywhere.
questions.explanation_th: บอกความต้องการเรื่องสถานที่

options: Time means nothing to me ever.
A week has exactly seven days.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบระยะเวลาที่จะพัก

options: That sounds perfect. How much does it cost?
I really don't care about Krabi.
questions.explanation_th: สนใจแล้วถามราคา

options: That is far too cheap for me.
questions.explanation_th: ตัดสินใจจองหลังรู้ราคา

longConversations.title: A student asks for advice
longConversations.text:
Teacher: Hi, come in. You wanted to talk about your project?
Student: __(1)__
Teacher: Of course. What seems to be the problem?
Student: __(2)__
Teacher: That's quite common. Have you tried narrowing your topic?
Student: __(3)__
Teacher: Good idea. I'd suggest focusing on just one city.
Student: __(4)__
Teacher: Yes, you can find plenty of sources online and in the library.
Student: __(5)__
Teacher: You're welcome. Good luck with it.

options: Yes, I'm having some trouble with it.
No, I have no project at all.
You wanted to talk to me.
questions.explanation_th: ยืนยันว่ามาปรึกษาปัญหา

options: I have no topic, so it's fine.
questions.explanation_th: อธิบายปัญหาของหัวข้อ

options: Not yet. How should I do that?
A topic is just a word.
No, I refuse to try that.
questions.explanation_th: ยังไม่ได้ทำ และถามวิธี

options: Do you think I can find enough information?
One city is far too many.
questions.explanation_th: ถามต่อเรื่องแหล่งข้อมูล

options: Thank you so much for your advice.
No thanks for the bad advice.
I will not thank you at all.
questions.explanation_th: ขอบคุณเมื่อจบการปรึกษา

longConversations.title: At the doctor's office
longConversations.text:
Doctor:  Good morning. What seems to be the problem today?
Patient: __(1)__
Doctor:  I'm sorry to hear that. Have you taken anything for it?
Patient: __(2)__
Doctor:  Are you sleeping and eating normally?
Patient: __(3)__
Doctor:  That could be the cause. I'd suggest more rest and plenty of water.
Patient: __(4)__
Doctor:  I don't think so, but come back if it gets worse.
Patient: __(5)__

options: I've had a bad headache for three days.
questions.explanation_th: บอกอาการเมื่อหมอถาม

options: Just some painkillers, but they didn't help much.
Yes, I hear you are sorry.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบว่ากินยาอะไรไปแล้ว

options: Not really. I've been very busy and stressed at work.
Yes, sleeping is for the night.
Normal is the opposite of strange.
questions.explanation_th: อธิบายพฤติกรรมการนอน/กิน

options: Should I be worried about anything serious?
Rest is a very nice thing.
questions.explanation_th: ถามว่าควรกังวลไหม
ขอบคุณและรับคำแนะนำ

longConversations.title: A job interview
longConversations.text:
Interviewer: Thanks for coming in. Could you tell me a little about yourself?
Candidate:   __(1)__
Interviewer: Great. Why do you want to work for our company?
Candidate:   __(2)__
Interviewer: What would you say is your biggest strength?
Candidate:   __(3)__
Interviewer: And do you have any questions for us?
Candidate:   __(4)__
Interviewer: Good question. We'll be in touch by next week.
Candidate:   __(5)__

options: Of course. I recently graduated in marketing and I enjoy working with people.
Telling is a form of speaking.
questions.explanation_th: แนะนำตัวเองสั้น ๆ

options: I admire your brand, and I think I could learn a lot here.

clozePassages.text: Most people know that drinking water is good __(1)__ them, but many of us still don't drink enough. readingPassages.title: Swimming pool closure notice
readingPassages.text:
NOTICE: Swimming Pool Closure

The community swimming pool will be closed from 1 to 5 March for its annual cleaning and repairs. During this time, our staff will replace the water filters and repaint the pool walls. We apologise for any inconvenience this may cause.

Members who have a monthly pass will have their membership extended by one week at no extra charge. The pool will reopen on 6 March at 7 a.m. with normal opening hours.

While the pool is closed, members are welcome to use the gym and the fitness classes as usual. If you have any questions, please contact the reception desk or call 02-345-6789.

Thank you for your understanding.
The Management

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

readingPassages.title: A morning walk
readingPassages.text:
Many people drink a cup of coffee to wake up in the morning, but did you know that a short walk can be just as effective? When we walk, our heart beats faster and sends more oxygen to the brain. This helps us feel more awake and ready to think clearly.

A morning walk has other benefits too. Spending a few minutes outdoors in natural light tells our body that the day has begun, which can improve our mood and help us sleep better at night. Walking is also gentle exercise that almost anyone can do, and it costs nothing.

Of course, this does not mean you must give up your morning coffee. But next time you feel sleepy, you might try going for a short walk first. You may be surprised at how refreshed you feel.

questions.stem: What is the main idea of the article?
options: A short morning walk can make you feel awake, much like coffee.
Coffee is bad for your health.
questions.explanation_th: ใจความหลัก: เดินตอนเช้าช่วยให้ตื่นตัวเหมือนกาแฟ

questions.stem: According to the text, what happens when we walk?
options: The heart sends more oxygen to the brain.
The brain stops working for a while.
questions.explanation_th: ระบุชัดในย่อหน้าแรก

questions.stem: What is one other benefit of a morning walk?
options: It can improve mood and help us sleep better.
It always costs a lot of money.
questions.explanation_th: ย่อหน้าสอง: อารมณ์ดีขึ้น+นอนหลับดีขึ้น

questions.stem: What does the writer suggest to the reader?
options: Try walking when you feel sleepy, not only drinking coffee.
Never drink coffee again in your life.
Walk for several hours each day.
Always stay indoors in the morning.
questions.explanation_th: อนุมานจากย่อหน้าสุดท้าย

questions.explanation_th: refreshed = สดชื่น มีพลังใหม่

readingPassages.title: Email about a wrong order
readingPassages.text:
From: David Lee
To: Customer Service
Subject: Problem with my online order

Dear Sir or Madam,

I am writing about my recent order (number 48213), which I received yesterday. Unfortunately, the jacket I ordered is the wrong size. I asked for a medium, but a small was delivered instead.

I have checked my order confirmation, and it clearly states that I selected a medium. The jacket itself is in perfect condition, so I would simply like to exchange it for the correct size.

Could you please tell me how to return the small jacket and arrange for a medium to be sent? I would be grateful if this could be done soon, as I would like to wear it on holiday next week.

I look forward to your reply.

Yours faithfully,
David Lee

questions.stem: Why did David write this email?
options: To exchange a jacket for the correct size.
To complain about a high price.
questions.explanation_th: จุดประสงค์: ขอเปลี่ยนไซซ์เสื้อ

questions.stem: What is wrong with the jacket David received?
questions.explanation_th: ระบุชัด: ส่งผิดไซซ์

questions.stem: What size did David originally order?
questions.explanation_th: เขายืนยันว่าเลือก medium

questions.stem: Why does David want the problem solved soon?
options: He wants to wear the jacket on holiday next week.
He is moving to a new house.
questions.explanation_th: อนุมานจากเหตุผลท้ายอีเมล

readingPassages.title: Cooking class advertisement
readingPassages.text:
Learn to Cook with Chef Mai!

Have you always wanted to cook delicious Thai food but never knew where to start? Join our weekend cooking classes at the Sukhumvit Cooking School and discover how easy it can be!

Our friendly teacher, Chef Mai, has over fifteen years of experience and will guide you step by step. Each class is small, with no more than eight students, so everyone gets plenty of attention. You will learn to prepare three classic dishes, and best of all, you get to eat what you make!

Classes run every Saturday and Sunday from 10 a.m. to 1 p.m. The price is 1,200 baht per class, which includes all ingredients and a recipe booklet to take home.

Spaces fill up quickly, so book online today at www.maicooking.com. We can't wait to cook with you!

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this text?
questions.explanation_th: จุดประสงค์: โฆษณาคลาสทำอาหาร

questions.explanation_th: ระบุ no more than eight

questions.stem: What is included in the price?
options: All ingredients and a recipe booklet.
questions.explanation_th: ระบุชัดในย่อหน้าราคา

questions.stem: Why does the text say "Spaces fill up quickly"?
options: To encourage people to book soon.

questions.explanation_th: guide = นำ/ช่วยแนะนำ

readingPassages.title: Using less plastic
readingPassages.text:
These days, many people are trying to use less plastic, and small changes can make a big difference. One of the easiest steps is to carry a reusable shopping bag. Plastic bags are used for only a few minutes but can take hundreds of years to break down.

Another simple idea is to use a refillable water bottle instead of buying bottled water. Not only does this reduce waste, but it also saves money over time. Many public places now have free water stations where you can refill your bottle.

Some people worry that living with less plastic is difficult or expensive. In fact, the opposite is often true. By reusing items and buying less, families can save money while helping the environment.

Of course, no one can be perfect. But if everyone makes a few small changes, together we can reduce the amount of plastic waste enormously.

questions.stem: What is the main idea of the article?
options: Small changes can help us use less plastic.
Bottled water is dangerous to drink.
questions.explanation_th: ใจความหลัก: การเปลี่ยนเล็ก ๆ ช่วยลดพลาสติก

questions.stem: How long can a plastic bag take to break down?

questions.stem: Besides reducing waste, what is one benefit of a refillable bottle?
questions.explanation_th: ระบุ saves money over time

questions.stem: What does the writer believe about living with less plastic?
options: It is usually easier and cheaper than people think.
questions.explanation_th: ผู้เขียนบอกว่าตรงข้ามกับที่คนกังวล

questions.stem: The word "enormously" is closest in meaning to ___.
questions.explanation_th: enormously = อย่างมหาศาล

readingPassages.title: Email: change of meeting time
readingPassages.text:
Subject: Change of meeting time

Hi everyone,

I'm writing to let you know that our team meeting, originally planned for Tuesday at 2 p.m., has been moved to Wednesday at 10 a.m. I'm sorry for the short notice. Unfortunately, the meeting room was double-booked on Tuesday, and Wednesday morning was the only time that worked for everyone.

The meeting will now take place in Room B on the second floor, not in our usual room. Please bring your progress reports, as we will be discussing the new project schedule.

If you cannot attend at the new time, please let me know as soon as possible so we can arrange a call instead.

Thanks for your understanding, and see you on Wednesday.
Best, Daniel

questions.stem: Why did Daniel write this email?
options: To inform the team that the meeting time has changed.
To invite new members to the team.
questions.explanation_th: จุดประสงค์: แจ้งเปลี่ยนเวลาประชุม

questions.stem: When will the meeting now take place?
questions.explanation_th: ระบุชัด

questions.stem: Why was the meeting moved?
options: No one could come on Tuesday.
questions.explanation_th: เหตุผล: ห้องถูกจองซ้อน

questions.stem: What should team members bring?
questions.explanation_th: ระบุ bring your progress reports

questions.stem: What should you do if you cannot attend?
options: Tell Daniel soon so a call can be arranged.
questions.explanation_th: อนุมานจากย่อหน้าท้าย

readingPassages.title: Notice: gym schedule change
readingPassages.text:
NOTICE TO ALL MEMBERS

Please note that, starting next Monday, the gym will be opening one hour earlier, at 6 a.m., on weekdays. We are making this change because many members have asked for more time to exercise before work.

At the same time, the Saturday closing time will move from 8 p.m. to 6 p.m. The gym will remain closed on Sundays, as usual, for cleaning and maintenance.

The new group fitness timetable is now available at the reception desk and on our website. We have added two new classes: morning yoga and evening cycling.

We hope these changes make it easier for you to fit exercise into your busy schedule. If you have any feedback, please speak to a member of staff.

Thank you, The Management

questions.stem: What is the notice mainly about?
options: Changes to the gym's opening hours and classes.
questions.explanation_th: ใจความหลัก: แจ้งเปลี่ยนเวลา+คลาส

questions.stem: What time will the gym open on weekdays from next Monday?
questions.explanation_th: ระบุ opening at 6 a.m.

questions.stem: Why is the gym opening earlier?
options: Members wanted more time to exercise before work.
questions.explanation_th: เหตุผลที่ระบุ

questions.stem: Which two new classes have been added?
questions.explanation_th: ระบุชัด

questions.stem: What can we understand about Sundays?
options: The gym stays closed for cleaning.
questions.explanation_th: อนุมานจาก remain closed on Sundays

readingPassages.title: A young inventor
readingPassages.text:
Somchai Pho is a young Thai inventor who has become well known for his clever, low-cost solutions to everyday problems. He grew up in a small village in the northeast, where his family farmed rice. As a child, he loved taking apart broken radios and toys to see how they worked.

After studying engineering at university, Somchai decided to return to his hometown instead of working in the city. He wanted to help local farmers, many of whom struggled with the high cost of equipment. His first invention was a simple, solar-powered water pump that cost far less than the ones in shops.

Today, Somchai runs a small workshop that trains young people in his community. He believes that good ideas do not need to be expensive. "The best inventions," he often says, "solve real problems for real people." His work has inspired many other young Thais to use their skills to help others.

questions.stem: What is the text mainly about?
options: A young inventor who creates low-cost solutions.
questions.explanation_th: ใจความหลัก: นักประดิษฐ์รุ่นใหม่ราคาประหยัด

questions.stem: What did Somchai enjoy doing as a child?
options: Taking apart broken radios and toys.
questions.explanation_th: ระบุในย่อหน้าแรก

questions.stem: What was his first invention?
questions.explanation_th: ระบุชัด

questions.stem: What does Somchai do today?
options: He runs a workshop that trains young people.
He farms rice with his family.
He works in a city office.
He sells radios in a shop.
questions.explanation_th: ระบุ runs a workshop

questions.stem: What does Somchai believe?
options: Good ideas don't have to be expensive.
Only rich people can invent things.
City jobs are always the best.
questions.explanation_th: อนุมานจากคำพูดของเขา

readingPassages.title: Product review: earphones
readingPassages.text:
I bought the SoundWave X earphones last month, and overall I'm very happy with them. The first thing I noticed was the sound quality, which is excellent for the price. The bass is strong without being too heavy, and voices are clear when I'm on a call.

The battery is another strong point. A single charge lasts about eight hours, which is more than enough for a day at work. The case charges them quickly too.

There are a few small downsides, however. The earphones don't fit my ears perfectly, so they can feel a little loose when I run. I also wish the app had more settings to adjust the sound.

Despite these minor problems, I would still recommend the SoundWave X to anyone looking for affordable, good-quality earphones. For the price, they offer great value, and I use them every single day.

questions.stem: What is the purpose of this text?
options: To review a pair of earphones.
questions.explanation_th: จุดประสงค์: รีวิวหูฟัง

questions.stem: How long does the battery last on one charge?
questions.explanation_th: ระบุ about eight hours

questions.stem: What is one downside the writer mentions?
options: The earphones can feel loose when running.
questions.explanation_th: ระบุ feel a little loose

questions.stem: What is the writer's overall opinion?
options: They are good value and worth recommending.
They are a complete waste of money.
They are the best in the whole world.
questions.explanation_th: อนุมานจากประโยคสรุป

questions.stem: The word "downsides" is closest in meaning to ___.
questions.explanation_th: downsides = ข้อเสีย

readingPassages.title: How to save money
readingPassages.text:
Saving money can feel difficult, but a few simple habits can help anyone build up their savings over time. The first step is to keep track of what you spend. Many people are surprised to find how much they spend on small things, like snacks or coffee, without noticing.

Once you know where your money goes, you can set a realistic budget. Experts suggest saving a small amount each month, even if it is just a little. The important thing is to do it regularly.

Another useful tip is to wait before making big purchases. If you still want something after a week, it is probably worth buying. If not, you have saved your money.

Finally, try to set a clear goal, such as saving for a trip or an emergency fund. Having a goal makes it much easier to stay motivated and avoid spending on things you don't really need.

questions.stem: What is the article mainly about?
options: Simple habits to help people save money.
How to earn a lot more money.
questions.explanation_th: ใจความหลัก: นิสัยง่าย ๆ ในการออม

questions.stem: What is the first step the writer suggests?
options: Keeping track of what you spend.
questions.explanation_th: ระบุ first step

questions.stem: What should you do before making a big purchase?
options: Wait for a week to see if you still want it.
questions.explanation_th: ระบุ wait before big purchases

questions.stem: Why does the writer suggest setting a goal?
options: It helps you stay motivated to save.
It makes you spend much more.
It is required by all banks.
It is the only way to earn money.
questions.explanation_th: อนุมานจากย่อหน้าสุดท้าย

questions.stem: The word "realistic" is closest in meaning to ___.
questions.explanation_th: realistic = สมเหตุสมผล/เป็นไปได้

readingPassages.title: Why bees matter
readingPassages.text:
Bees are small insects that play a surprisingly important role in our lives. As they move from flower to flower collecting nectar, they also carry pollen, which helps plants produce fruit and seeds. This process, called pollination, is essential for growing many of the foods we eat every day.

In recent years, however, the number of bees in many parts of the world has fallen. Scientists believe this is partly due to the loss of wild flowers, the use of certain chemicals, and changes in the weather. If bee numbers continue to drop, it could become harder and more expensive to grow some crops.

Fortunately, there are simple things people can do to help. Planting flowers, avoiding harmful chemicals in gardens, and supporting local beekeepers can all make a difference. By protecting bees, we are also protecting the food supply that so many people depend on.

questions.stem: What is the article mainly about?
options: Why bees are important and how to help them.
How to make honey at home.
Why bees are dangerous to people.
questions.explanation_th: ใจความหลัก: ความสำคัญของผึ้ง+วิธีช่วย

questions.stem: What is pollination?
options: When bees carry pollen, helping plants make fruit and seeds.
When bees make honey in a hive.
When bees sting people in gardens.
questions.explanation_th: นิยามในย่อหน้าแรก

questions.stem: What is one reason bee numbers have fallen?
options: The loss of wild flowers and use of chemicals.
questions.explanation_th: ระบุสาเหตุ

questions.stem: What might happen if bee numbers keep dropping?
options: Some crops could be harder and more expensive to grow.
questions.explanation_th: อนุมานจากย่อหน้าสอง

questions.stem: The word "essential" is closest in meaning to ___.
questions.explanation_th: essential = จำเป็นอย่างยิ่ง

readingPassages.title: A small act of kindness
readingPassages.text:
When Lara moved to a new city for her job, she felt lonely at first. She didn't know anyone, and the busy streets felt strange and unfriendly. Every evening, she would go straight home and stay in her small flat.

One Saturday, feeling bored, she decided to visit a local market she had walked past many times. As she looked at the colourful fruit and handmade crafts, an elderly woman at one stall smiled and offered her a piece of mango to try. They began to chat, and the woman told Lara about a community art class held nearby.

Lara decided to give it a try. At the class, she met people who shared her interests, and slowly the city began to feel like home. Looking back, she realised that one small moment of kindness had changed everything. Now, she always tries to make newcomers feel welcome too.

questions.stem: What is the story mainly about?
options: How a small act of kindness helped someone feel at home.
How to find a good job.
questions.explanation_th: ใจความหลัก: น้ำใจเล็ก ๆ เปลี่ยนทุกอย่าง

questions.stem: How did Lara feel when she first moved to the city?
questions.explanation_th: ระบุ felt lonely at first

questions.stem: Who offered Lara a piece of mango?
options: An elderly woman at a market stall.
questions.explanation_th: ระบุชัด

questions.stem: What did Lara do after hearing about the art class?
questions.explanation_th: ระบุ decided to give it a try

questions.stem: What does Lara do now?
options: She tries to make newcomers feel welcome.
She stays at home every day.
She has moved to another city.
questions.explanation_th: อนุมานจากประโยคสุดท้าย
